methodsAttempted.query: site:asu.edu Arizona State University generative AI policy students faculty ChatGPT academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search surfaced ASU Provost, ASU AI portal, ASU Library, school guidance, and official news/course-specific pages.
Second official-domain search confirmed central Provost FAQs, the ASU AI policy/resources page, and library guidance.

methodsAttempted.query: site:provost.asu.edu OR site:teachonline.asu.edu ASU "generative AI" syllabus teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Syllabus discovery found the central Provost page and several individual course PDFs, which were rejected as course-specific evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: site:asu.edu "AI tools" "data" "Arizona State University" "generative AI" privacy security
methodsAttempted.notes: Data and security search found the official ASU AI tools page and the Research Compliance artificial intelligence page.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found claim-ready ASU evidence on Provost, ai.asu.edu, Research Compliance, ASU Library, and an ASU School of Life Sciences teaching toolkit page. Official news, individual course PDFs, and an externally hosted Google Doc were rejected or not used as policy evidence.

artifacts.title: Arizona State University generative AI staged source review
artifacts.summary: Staged review of official Arizona State University sources found central guidance from the Provost, ASU Artificial Intelligence, Research Compliance, and ASU Library pages. Claims cover approved tools, FERPA-oriented tool support, student instructor-check guidance, digital trust/privacy expectations, research-data restrictions, AI acknowledgement, and one school-toolkit-scoped syllabus communication point.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Course permission for student AI use remains instructor- and assignment-specific under the cited ASU Provost FAQ.
The syllabus communication claim is scoped to the ASU School of Life Sciences teaching toolkit page, not a university-wide mandate.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
